metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **Locked design / S4 (CONTRIBUTING.md merge-back recipe):** add an explicit step to install the Gerrit `commit-msg` hook in the merge working tree before the `git commit --amend` that stamps the Change-Id (e.g. `curl -Lo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://<host>/tools/hooks/commit-msg && chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg`).
findings.finding: The merge-back recipe in the Locked design section stamps a Change-Id onto the `--no-ff` merge commit using `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend`. This depends on the Gerrit `commit-msg` hook being installed in the working tree at amend time; `git commit --amend` does not inject a Change-Id on its own — the hook must be present and executable. The plan does not specify whether the agent's working tree (or a scripted step) ensures the hook is installed before the amend, leaving a gap where the push to `refs/for/main` would be rejected by Gerrit with 'missing Change-Id'.
findings.location: Locked design — Merge-back subsection: 'git merge --no-ff merge commit (Change-Id stamped via GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the CONTRIBUTING.md merge-back recipe and the Locked design description, prepend the amend step with: 'ensure the commit-msg hook is installed in the working tree (curl -sLo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://<gerrit-host>/tools/hooks/commit-msg && chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg)' before `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the cost/latency note criterion observable: specify the artifact location (e.g. a section in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` or the ADR), the metrics to capture (e.g. median CI wall-clock per merge change, vote-round-trips avoided per story, measured from S5 scenario run), and the minimum content required so the criterion has a deterministic done-check.
findings.finding: S6's 'cost/latency notes recorded' criterion is effort-framed and non-observable: it specifies neither what metrics to measure, what acceptable values look like, nor where the notes live — so there is no deterministic in-session check that the criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out, 'cost/latency notes recorded'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `voter.py` explicitly to the S2 edit-target list and specify that `review_and_vote` must detect merge commits (via parent count) before calling `get_patch`, dispatching to the new merge-change assembly path instead.
findings.finding: voter.py line 216 calls `gc.get_patch(change_id, revision)` unconditionally — this is the exact 409 path the plan identifies as a blocker for merge changes, yet voter.py is not named as a required edit target in S2 (only 'src/rebar/review_bot/' is named generically). The merge-change detection and dispatch logic must fork before this call, which means voter.py itself needs modification.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path; src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:216
findings.suggested_fix: In S2 AC, replace 'src/rebar/review_bot/' with an explicit list: voter.py (branch before get_patch call), gerrit_client.py (new get_commit, get_files_diff, get_mergelist methods), and tests/unit/test_review_bot.py (new merge-change fixtures).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2 — Verify `GET files/` default parent for merge commits on Gerrit 3.14.1**: before implementing the merge-change review-input assembly, either (a) add a captured Gerrit REST response fixture from the running instance proving `files/` (no `parent`) returns the auto-merge-base diff for a merge commit, or (b) cite the specific Gerrit 3.14.1 release note / REST API doc section that pins this behavior, and add a unit test with a fixture that asserts the auto-merge-base file list is used (not first-parent).
findings.finding: The plan's S2 bot path for merge changes is built on the claim that `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files/` with no `parent` parameter defaults to the auto-merge base for merge commits. This is the architectural foundation for assembling the review input ("files/ with no parent → auto-merge base"), but the assertion is stated without a pinned Gerrit 3.14.1 release-note citation, without an in-repo captured response fixture, and without a test validating the behavior against the running instance. The current gerrit_client.py has no `files/` code path at all — the entire claim is untested against the live Gerrit version.
findings.location: Decision log — 'Merge-change review base = Gerrit auto-merge'; S2 AC (`src/rebar/review_bot/`) — 'assemble review input from `files/` (no `parent` → auto-merge base)'
findings.suggested_fix: In S2, add a `get_merge_files(change_id, revision)` method to `gerrit_client.py` that calls `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files` (no parent), and create a fixture test capturing a real Gerrit 3.14.1 response for a merge commit. The Gerrit REST API doc (`rest-api-changes.html#list-files`) states: 'For merge commits, the default is the auto-merge'; add a direct citation of that section with the version anchor to the S2 implementation note.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reconcile.py` explicitly to the S2 edit-target list, noting that `_candidate_events` must propagate merge-change events (already present in the events-log) to the new merge-change path in voter.py.
findings.finding: reconcile.py's `_candidate_events` function filters events by project but has no awareness of merge commits — it will surface merge-change patchset-created events the same as regular changes and route them through `voter.review_and_vote`. If S2 adds a merge-change path to voter.py but reconcile.py is not updated, the reconciler's backfill path may call voter.py with merge events before the merge-change branch is in place, hitting the 409 path. The plan mentions 'reconcile.py backfill and /rerun work for merge changes' but does not name reconcile.py as an S2 edit target.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path; src/rebar/review_bot/reconcile.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2** Specify explicitly that merge-change detection (the `revisions/{rev}/commit` parent-count API call) occurs inside `voter.review_and_vote` on every review path — webhook, reconciler backfill, and `/rerun` — so the merge path is transparent to callers and no event enrichment is required from the reconciler.
findings.finding: S2 requires 'NEVER bare `/patch` on merges (assert the 409 path stays fail-closed if hit)' and 'reconcile.py backfill and `/rerun` work for merge changes', but the plan does not specify how the reconciler's patchset-created–shaped event causes the voter to follow the merge path vs. the regular path. The voter must detect merge changes via `revisions/{rev}/commit` parent-count, but the reconciler synthesizes events from the events-log without a merge-marker — the plan is silent on whether this detection happens inside `voter.review_and_vote` (transparent) or requires the reconciler to emit enriched events.
findings.location: S2 — 'reconcile.py backfill and `/rerun` work for merge changes'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to S2: 'Merge detection (`revisions/{rev}/commit` parent count ≥ 2) is performed inside `voter.review_and_vote` immediately after the existing-vote check, before any diff fetch, so the merge path is taken identically regardless of whether the review was triggered by a webhook, the reconciler, or `/rerun`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the S4 CONTRIBUTING merge-back recipe, explicitly add 'install the `commit-msg` hook before creating the merge commit' as a required prerequisite step (mirroring the reviewbot-e2e.sh pattern: `curl -Lo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://<host>/tools/hooks/commit-msg && chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg`), and add a troubleshooting entry for 'missing Change-Id on merge push'.
findings.finding: The merge-back recipe in the Locked design section specifies `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend` to stamp a Change-Id on the `--no-ff` merge commit, but this only works if the Gerrit `commit-msg` hook is installed in the worktree where the merge is created. Agents operating in fresh worktrees — the core multi-agent use case — will not have the hook unless it is explicitly installed beforehand. Without it, `git commit --amend` completes silently without adding a Change-Id, and Gerrit rejects the push with 'missing Change-Id in commit message footer': a non-obvious failure that looks like a misconfiguration rather than a missing recipe step. The existing `reviewbot-e2e.sh` (lines 145–149) shows the correct pattern of fetching the hook before any commit, but the merge-back recipe stub does not yet include this prerequisite.
findings.location: Locked design — Merge-back bullet (`--no-ff` merge commit + `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend`); S4 — CONTRIBUTING.md merge-back recipe
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the CONTRIBUTING merge-back recipe, before the `git merge --no-ff` step: `curl -sS -Lo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/tools/hooks/commit-msg && chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg` (or `git-review -s` if git-review is available). Also note in the S4 CLAUDE.md feature-branch guidance that agents should install the hook in any fresh worktree before the merge-back step.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the conditional 'document any g2p branch-filter config required' with the specific determination: either state the required g2p branch-filter config (e.g. `branch_filter: refs/heads/feature/.*` in the hook config) or explicitly record 'no branch-filter change needed because g2p hooks are already branch-agnostic' so the executor has a verifiable target state.
findings.finding: S3 contains a conditional discovery bullet ('document any g2p branch-filter config required') that defers a real configuration decision to the executor rather than stating the outcome or the default.
findings.location: S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2** Define the exact schema/format of the merge-change context string passed to `produce_code_review_verdict` — specify how the auto-merge diff, per-file diff entries, and `mergelist` commit summaries are assembled into the `diff_text` (or a new named parameter), and co-locate this contract with the `DiffContext`/`assemble_diff_context` interface.
findings.finding: S2 specifies assembling merge-change review input from `files/` + per-file diffs + `mergelist` context, but never defines the schema/format of the combined string passed to `produce_code_review_verdict(diff_text=...)`. The existing code path passes a single unified diff string; the plan references a new 'merged' context but leaves its structure (how mergelist entries are formatted, whether they precede or follow the auto-merge diff, encoding) undefined and not co-located with the `DiffContext`/`produce_code_review_verdict` interface that must consume it.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path (`src/rebar/review_bot/`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff: str, mergelist: list[dict]) -> str` function (or `MergeChangeDiffContext` dataclass) to `assemble.py` with a documented format (e.g. a `## Merge Context` section listing integrated commits, followed by `## Auto-merge Diff` with the actual diff), then reference it explicitly in S2's AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2** Specify the size-cap strategy for merge-change context: define whether `DIFF_CHAR_CAP` applies to the combined string or to each section (auto-merge diff vs. mergelist context) separately, and define any per-mergelist-entry truncation rule (e.g. max N commits, max M chars per commit summary).
findings.finding: S2 specifies 'merge-context size-capped like DIFF_CHAR_CAP' but does not define whether the cap applies to (a) only the auto-merge diff portion, (b) only the mergelist context portion, or (c) the combined assembled string. The mergelist context format is also unspecified, making it impossible to implement a correct cap without arbitrary decisions.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path, size-cap specification

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between Locked Design ('g2p hooks are branch-agnostic') and S3 ('document any g2p branch-filter config required'): read the g2p source or docs to determine whether gerrit_to_platform.ini requires explicit branch patterns to fire workflow_dispatch on feature/* refs, and replace the hedge in S3 with a concrete, verified statement of the required config (or a no-config-needed confirmation).
findings.finding: The Locked Design section asserts 'the review-bot webhook and g2p hooks are branch-agnostic' as a settled, present-tense fact. The webhook half is verifiable (webhooks.config has no branch filter — confirmed). However, g2p is not yet installed (epic 1fa8 is open/incomplete), so the g2p-hook side of this claim is unverifiable from the codebase. Worse, S3 itself hedges: 'document any g2p branch-filter config required' — directly contradicting the settled-fact framing in Locked Design. If g2p does require branch-filter configuration for feature/* dispatching, the design's R3 guarantee ('every change reaching main passed LLM review') could silently break on feature-branch changes.
findings.location: Locked Design paragraph ('the review-bot webhook and g2p hooks are branch-agnostic') vs. S3 AC ('document any g2p branch-filter config required')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3 / Locked design:** resolve the g2p branch-filter ambiguity before marking it 'settled' — verify whether `gerrit_to_platform.ini` requires explicit branch/project patterns for `feature/*` targets or dispatches unconditionally, and add the confirmed config (or a confirmed no-op) to the `gerrit_to_platform.ini` spec in S3.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the review-bot webhook and g2p hooks are branch-agnostic' as a settled fact in the Locked design section, but S3 immediately hedges with 'document any g2p branch-filter config required.' The `gerrit_to_platform.ini` configuration can include project and branch filter settings; if g2p silently suppresses `workflow_dispatch` for `refs/heads/feature/*` targets (e.g. due to a missing branch pattern or a project-filter mismatch), feature-branch story changes will never receive a Verified vote — violating R3 with no visible error signal. The plan does not resolve whether g2p fires unconditionally for all branches or needs filter config.
findings.location: Locked design section ('the review-bot webhook and g2p hooks are branch-agnostic') and S3 AC ('document any g2p branch-filter config required')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit verification step to S3: check the g2p `gerrit_to_platform.ini` docs for `[gerrit] projects` / branch-filter keys; if branch-agnostic dispatch is confirmed, record that in S3 (remove the hedge); if filter config is needed, add the required `feature/*` pattern to the ini spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2** Define the robot comment template/schema for merge-change reviews: specify the tag prefix (e.g. extend `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` with a `merge-change` variant), the integrated-commit count format, and whether this is expressed as a new tag suffix, a preamble line, or a structured field in the robot comment body.
findings.finding: S2 states the bot's robot comment 'states merge-change mode + integrated-commit count', but no schema or template for this comment is defined. The existing `post_vote` in `gerrit_client.py` writes a flat `message` string with fixed `robot_id`/`robot_run_id` fields. The plan does not specify whether 'merge-change mode' is a tag prefix (like the existing `[LLM-Review: PASS]` tag in adapter.py's `_TAG_SUFFIXES`), a separate field, or free text — nor what 'integrated-commit count' looks like in the comment.
findings.location: S2 — 'robot comment states merge-change mode + integrated-commit count'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` with a merge-change mode indicator and add a comment preamble template, e.g.: `'[LLM-Review: PASS (merge-change, N integrated commits)]\n<summary>'`, co-located with the existing tag vocabulary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the contradiction between 'g2p hooks are branch-agnostic' (Locked design) and 'document any g2p branch-filter config required' (S3): either confirm branch-agnosticism as a pre-condition (and add a verification step), or replace the assertion with 'verify and configure' and add a concrete S3 done-definition that g2p dispatches for feature/* refs.
findings.finding: The Locked design section asserts 'the review-bot webhook and g2p hooks are branch-agnostic' as a settled fact, but S3 hedges by saying 'document any g2p branch-filter config required' — implying it is NOT yet confirmed. An implementer reading the Locked design would skip g2p configuration investigation; one reading S3 would investigate it. If g2p has a branch filter that excludes feature/*, CI will silently not run on feature-branch story changes, breaking R3.
findings.location: Locked design section ('g2p hooks are branch-agnostic') vs S3 AC ('document any g2p branch-filter config required')
findings.suggested_fix: In Locked design, change 'are branch-agnostic' to 'are expected to be branch-agnostic (verified in S3)'. In S3, add an explicit done-definition: 'a feature/* patchset-created event triggers gerrit-verify.yaml and Verified is cast — confirmed by log evidence in E2E run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S2, specify that the merge-change bot path emits structured log events (and, if the existing system has a metrics layer, counters/gauges) for each new failure mode: 409-guard firing, mergelist fetch error, parent-count detection result, and merge-change-mode activation — so operators can alert on and diagnose stalls without runbook spelunking.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new failure modes specific to the merge-change path (409-guard firing in production, mergelist fetch failure, parent-count detection returning unexpected data) but does not specify any instrumentation — metrics, structured log events, or alerts — for these new failure points. The runbook update (S4) describes operational procedures after a problem is noticed, but operators have no proactive signal that, e.g., every merge change is silently fail-closing because the commit endpoint is returning malformed data.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path; S4 — infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md
findings.suggested_fix: In voter.py / gerrit_client.py, emit a tagged log line (e.g. `logger.warning('merge_change_409_guard_fired', change_id=..., revision=...)`) for each guard/error branch in the new merge-change path, and add a corresponding entry to the runbook's 'signals to watch' section. If the system already has a Prometheus/CloudWatch metric layer, add `merge_change_errors_total{reason=...}` and wire an alert for sustained non-zero rate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T10(g)** Add structured observability for the S2 merge-change path: at minimum, a counter/log tag distinguishing merge-change reviews from regular reviews, an explicit log/alert when the 409 fail-closed branch is hit, and a metric for merge-change review outcomes — reference these in the runbook update.
findings.finding: The new merge-change code path in the review bot (S2) introduces multiple new failure modes (409 fallback, auto-merge-base diff assembly, mergelist fetch) but the plan specifies no new operational observability — no metrics, counters, or alerts for these paths beyond updating the prose runbook.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path; S4 — `infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md`
findings.suggested_fix: In S2 acceptance criteria, add: "review-bot emits structured log events `{event: merge_change_review, change_id, integrated_commits_count}` and `{event: merge_change_409_fallback, change_id}` (the latter at ERROR level); `infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` references these log patterns for alerting."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S1 / Locked Design (least-privilege)**: Specify the group (e.g., `feature-merge-drivers` or the same group that holds `Create Reference`) that receives `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` and `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*`, and document the membership-control policy in the ACL section and the ADR amendment.
findings.finding: The `Push Merge Commit` ACL granted on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` and `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*` does not specify which group receives the permission. Unlike `Create Reference`/`Delete Reference` (both attributed to 'a narrow group'), `Push Merge Commit` is listed without a group binding in both the Locked Design and S1 AC. If this grant is applied too broadly (e.g., to all registered users or all project members), any authenticated Gerrit user could initiate a merge change against main — widening the attack surface even though label gates still protect submission.
findings.location: Locked Design ('Push Merge Commit' bullet) and S1 AC ('ACLs: … Push Merge Commit on refs/for/refs/heads/main + refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*')
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/gerrit/project.config` and the ADR, bind `Push Merge Commit` to the same named narrow group that holds `Create Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` (e.g., `group feature-branch-drivers`). If broader participation is intended (any committer can propose a merge-back), state that explicitly with the rationale that label gates are the enforcement point, so the ACL is intentionally wide.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S1, document the CURRENT inherited submit-type value (query `project.config` or Gerrit inheritance chain before the change), confirm transition safety for any in-flight main changes, and add a rollback note specifically for the submit-type pin (distinct from the ACL rollback already in S6).
findings.finding: The plan pins `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` as a fix for an 'unpinned gap' — meaning the current production submit type is unspecified/inherited. If main currently uses FF-only or rebase-always, this S1 config change is a one-shot behavioral flip affecting ALL in-flight and future submissions on main with no staged rollout, no flag, and no specified old-value→new-value transition check. The plan specifies deploy ordering between S1 and S2 (bot before merge changes) but not between the submit-type change and any in-flight changes on main that were queued under the old behavior.
findings.location: S1 — Gate + ACL config; Decision log 'Submit type pinned explicitly' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Before applying the `[submit]` block, run `ssh gerrit gerrit ls-projects -d <project>` or inspect the inherited `All-Projects` config to record the current effective submit type. If it is currently FF or cherry-pick, schedule the pin during a quiet window with no open changes targeting main, and add a one-line note in the S6 back-out section: 'to revert submit-type pin, remove the [submit] block and verify inherited value matches prior behavior'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S2, specify whether DIFF_CHAR_CAP should be imported from `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` or re-declared as a local constant in the review_bot package, and name the specific file where the cap logic will live.
findings.finding: The plan states the merge-context diff will be 'size-capped like DIFF_CHAR_CAP' (S2), but DIFF_CHAR_CAP is defined in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` — outside the review_bot package. The plan does not enumerate this cross-package dependency or specify whether the constant should be imported from assemble.py or re-declared locally in the bot.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path
findings.suggested_fix: Re-declare the constant locally in gerrit_client.py or a new merge_review.py helper (e.g. `_MERGE_DIFF_CHAR_CAP = 60_000`) to avoid a cross-package dependency from the bot into the LLM layer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific Gerrit group (or groups) that will hold `Create Reference`/`Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` and `Push Merge Commit` on the relevant refs, so an executor can write the exact `group` lines in project.config without guessing.
findings.finding: The ACL group granted `Create Reference`/`Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` is described only as 'a narrow group' — the actual Gerrit group name is never specified, so an executor implementing `project.config` cannot fill in the group name without stopping to decide or ask.
findings.location: Locked design — ACL section; S1 acceptance criterion
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'ACLs granted to the existing `<Project>-Maintainers` group (same group that holds `Submit` on refs/heads/main)' — or create and name a new `Feature-Branch-Managers` group — directly in the Locked Design ACL section and S1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the S6 back-out procedure to explicitly include reverting `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` in project.config (or document that the inherited default is already MERGE_IF_NECESSARY and therefore the pin is a no-op change, making rollback moot).
findings.finding: The back-out procedure in S6 states 'revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes', but it does not mention reverting the `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` pin in project.config. That pin changes submit behavior for ALL changes on main (not just feature-branch merges), and omitting it from the rollback path leaves the back-out incomplete — the single-change-only flow could silently operate under a different submit strategy than before the epic.
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out; S1 — `[submit]` block pinning
findings.suggested_fix: In S6, add: 'If the submit-type was previously unset/inherited, also revert the [submit] block in project.config to its prior state; if the inherited default was already MERGE_IF_NECESSARY, document this explicitly so operators know the pin is safe to leave in place.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-flight check in S1 that verifies the Verified label block exists in the running Gerrit (or in project.config) before applying the copyCondition amendment, and gate S1 delivery on 1fa8 being confirmed merged.
findings.finding: The plan assumes `Verified` label and `gerrit-verify.yaml` exist as live infrastructure ('the Verified label exists, cast by GitHub Actions'), but the current project.config has no Verified label and .github/workflows/ has no gerrit-verify.yaml. S1's copyCondition edit for Verified and S3's CI coverage work are silently contingent on epic 1fa8 landing first; if 1fa8 is still in progress when this epic starts, S1 will write a copyCondition for a label that does not yet exist, which Gerrit will silently ignore rather than reject.
findings.location: S1 — Gate + ACL config; Context ('ASSUMES epic 1fa8 is completed and merged')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to S1 (e.g. `ssh -p 29418 gerrit gerrit ls-projects -p <project> --show-branch-sha1` + `GET /a/projects/<project>/config` asserting `submit_type=MERGE_IF_NECESSARY` and `GET /a/changes/?q=label:LLM-Review+copyCondition` or equivalent) so 'Verified live' has a deterministic pass/fail check rather than relying on absence of error.
findings.finding: S1's completion criterion 'Verified live on the running Gerrit' has no concrete proving command — an executor cannot deterministically confirm that the new submit requirements, label copy conditions, and submit-type pin are all active without a specified REST call or `ssh gerrit` query.
findings.location: S1 acceptance criterion — final bullet 'Verified live on the running Gerrit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an S5 scenario (e.g. scenario 8) that validates the catch-up merge flow: push a reviewed merge change of main into `feature/e2e-<date>`, confirm both LLM-Review and Verified gate it, confirm it submits and the branch history shows the merge, and confirm MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE does NOT trigger (it's a branch target, not a merge-back).
findings.finding: Catch-up merges (main→feature as reviewed merge changes on the branch) are described in the Locked design and their recipe is documented in S4, but no S5 E2E scenario validates this flow end-to-end. This is a described deliverable with no test coverage criterion.
findings.location: Locked design ('Catch-up merges main→feature as reviewed merge changes on the branch, on a documented cadence') vs S5 scenario list (scenarios 1–7)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Standardize all refspec references for the merge push target to the canonical Gerrit ACL form used in project.config (likely `refs/for/refs/heads/main`) and update Context/Decision log shorthand references with a parenthetical '(shorthand for refs/for/refs/heads/main)' to make the normative form unambiguous.
findings.finding: The refspec for pushing merge changes to main is written two different ways: 'refs/for/main' in Context and Decision log, and 'refs/for/refs/heads/main' in S1 and Locked design. In Gerrit ACL config the exact string matters; an implementer writing the pushMerge ACL entry using one form may get a subtly different (or broken) rule than intended.
findings.location: Context section ('pushed to refs/for/main'); Decision log ('pushed to refs/for/main as its own reviewed change'); Locked design ('Push Merge Commit on refs/for/refs/heads/main'); S1 ('Push Merge Commit on refs/for/refs/heads/main + refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*')
findings.suggested_fix: Pick `refs/for/refs/heads/main` as the normative form (it matches Gerrit's project.config ACL syntax) and annotate the two shorthand uses in Context/Decision log as '(shorthand)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2 / ADR — Verify or caveat the `patch?parent=1` behavior claim**: either test `GET /patch?parent=1` against a merge commit on the running Gerrit 3.14.1 and record the actual response (whole-feature diff, 409, or other), or caveat the decision log / ADR to note this is unverified and the `files/`-based path is chosen independently of this assertion.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch?parent=1` 'silently yields the ENTIRE feature diff (violates R1)' — this is used as the justification for never calling `/patch` with a parent parameter on merge changes. This behavior is stated as fact but is not verified against the running Gerrit 3.14.1 instance, and no in-repo test or fixture documents what `patch?parent=1` actually returns on a merge commit.
findings.location: Decision log — 'TRAP: bare `GET .../revisions/{rev}/patch` on a merge commit returns HTTP 409 … `patch?parent=1` silently yields the ENTIRE feature diff (violates R1)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S6 (or S4 CONTRIBUTING.md), define the cancellation/abandonment retirement path for feature branches: who is responsible, how to abandon open changes on the branch (Gerrit abandon API), and how to delete the ref, including a note that back-out of ACLs should be preceded by deletion of any active feature branches.
findings.finding: The retirement path for a feature branch that is CANCELLED or abandoned mid-development (never merged back) is not explicitly defined. The plan specifies deletion only as a post-merge step ('Branch deletion after merge-back is a scripted/manual step'), and S6 back-out addresses revoking ACLs but does not enumerate cleanup of already-created orphaned branches. Without a defined cancellation path, feature branches can accumulate indefinitely in Gerrit.
findings.location: Locked design — 'Branch deletion after merge-back is a scripted/manual step'; S6 — Process + back-out

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the S5 multi-agent E2E script extends / sources `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`'s existing SSH/REST/SSM helpers or is a new standalone script; if standalone, extract the shared infrastructure (SSM token fetch, SSH wrapper, `api_get`, vote-polling loop, harness/blocked/pass exit taxonomy) into a sourced `infra/gerrit/e2e-lib.sh` so both scripts share one copy.
findings.finding: S5 says the multi-agent E2E scenarios are 'scripted alongside `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`', but the existing script (~333 lines) already implements the full SSH/REST/SSM infrastructure substrate (bot-token fetch from SSM, admin SSH wrapper, XSSI-stripped Gerrit REST helper, vote-polling loop, harness-vs-gate exit-code taxonomy). If 'alongside' means a new, independent script that re-implements those helpers for the 7 new scenarios rather than extending or `source`-ing the existing script, that is NIH duplication of live operational scaffolding.
findings.location: S5 — Live multi-agent E2E validation / 'scripted alongside `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`'
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the reusable infrastructure from `reviewbot-e2e.sh` (lines ~93-116, 196-208) into `infra/gerrit/e2e-lib.sh` (sourced by both scripts); the existing script becomes `source e2e-lib.sh` + its single-change scenario logic, and the new multi-agent script sources the same lib for its 7 scenarios.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T10(d)** Add a pre-production validation step for the S1 config changes — either a staging Gerrit instance, a `git diff`-reviewed `project.config` PR with explicit before/after ACL review, or at minimum a documented rollback procedure (e.g. revert commit + push to `All-Projects`) tied to the S1 acceptance criterion.
findings.finding: The plan applies ACL changes and a submit-strategy change (`action = merge if necessary`) directly to the running production Gerrit with no staging or pre-production environment mentioned. The submit-type change in particular affects ALL in-flight changes on main, not just feature-branch work.
findings.location: S1 — Gate + ACL config; Sequencing & risk section
findings.suggested_fix: In S1, add an explicit sub-step: "stage config changes in a test project or All-Projects draft branch, verify ACL/submit-type behaviour with a throw-away change before applying to the main project.config; commit the project.config change as a reviewable Gerrit change itself (All-Projects is a Git repo) so the two-vote gate applies to the infra change too."

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
